programme.desc: World War II was a large and deadly conflict; from 1939 until 1945, more than fifty nations engaged in harrowing combat, and while the Axis Powers were eventually defeated, plenty of mysteries emerged from the rubble.

programme.title: The UnXplained Special Presentation
programme.desc: Most disappearances can be explained by either foul play or bad luck. However, there are some cases that defy conventional wisdom. How can a modern jetliner simply drop off radar screens? And why do so many people go missing in the state of Alaska? What really happened to a famous explorer who disappeared deep in the jungles of the Amazon? Could there be strange forces behind these vanishings?

programme.title: The UnXplained Special Presentation
programme.desc: An investigation into what drives certain people to deceive and execute outrageous and daring plots; a look into how some elaborate crimes, from elusive imposters disguised as foreign doctors or socialites, to dangerous killers.

programme.title: The UnXplained Special Presentation
programme.desc: How people develop extraordinary abilities, like remote viewing or enduring extreme temperatures by using one's mind.

programme.title: The UnXplained Special Presentation
programme.desc: Humanity has long been fascinated with the galactic worlds beyond our planet; analyzing what mysteries are yet to be solved about the surface of the Moon and what would happen to Earth if the Moon disappears; discussing if the impact craters and massive scar on Mars reveal a cataclysmic history on the barren Red Planet.

programme.title: The UnXplained Special Presentation
programme.desc: Believers have researched texts and studied histories throughout centuries, not only to strengthen their faith, but to find deeper meanings and profound answers to existence itself.

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd

programme.title: The UnBelievable With Dan Aykroyd
programme.desc: Uncover the wild beginnings behind ordinary objects, legendary figures, and even the sayings we use every day. Why did Abraham Lincoln once step into the wrestling ring? programme.title: One Foot in the Grave
programme.desc: The immensely moving final episode of the long-running sitcom, in which uber-grump Victor finally meets his maker. David Renwick leaves us with a twist.

programme.title: One Foot in the Grave: The Man Who Blew Away
programme.desc: An extended special of the superb comedy with Richard Wilson. Victor is visited by the depressive Mr Foskett, played by another sitcom legend, Brian Murphy.

programme.desc: More unlikely quirks of fate conspire to torment retired curmudgeon Victor Meldrew in the classic comedy. A routine medical check-up has sinister implications.
